positional_bias.notes: Positions re-derived from gift: q1=0, q2=1, q3=2, q4=3, q5=0, q6=1. Matches sidecar exactly (no desync). max_share 2/6=0.333 <= 0.40. PASS.

length_bias.methodology: Lengths measured from gift after stripping leading = or ~ and all text from first # onward (inclusive). Mean computed over the 3 distractor answer texts only. Correct flagged if correct_len > mean_distractor_len * 1.20.

per_question_breakdown.correct_text_stripped: The geometric path from one word-point to another; when shared by many pairs, it encodes a relationship.

d1.text: The count of dimensions that separate two word vectors in the shared embedding space.

d2.text: The similarity score between two words, as computed by the dot product during training.

d3.text: The axis label the model assigns to each dimension when the embedding is first built.

per_question_breakdown.note: 104 > 102.0 — correct answer exceeds 120% of distractor mean by 2 chars. Marginal.

per_question_breakdown.correct_text_stripped: Start at king, subtract the man vector to remove the male component, add the woman vector for the female component, then find the nearest vocabulary word.

d0.text: Start at king, add the woman vector first to form a female-royalty concept, then subtract the man vector and compare directly to the queen vector.

d2.text: Compute the dot product between king and queen, then subtract the dot product between man and woman to derive the final analogy result.

d3.text: Subtract the woman vector from king, then add the man vector, and round the result to the nearest whole-number coordinate in the space.

per_question_breakdown.correct_text_stripped: Training noise and king's extra contexts shift the result near queen but not exactly on it; nearest-word search gives the final answer.

d0.text: The arithmetic is approximate because vector addition and subtraction are not fully defined when the embedding has more than two dimensions.

d1.text: It is approximate because the dot product rounds each coordinate value to the nearest integer before computing the final similarity score.

d3.text: The arithmetic is not approximate; the result is always exact when all three words appeared frequently enough in the training corpus.

per_question_breakdown.correct_text_stripped: actor − man + woman ≈ actress, and Paris − France + Germany ≈ Berlin — both strip one property and add another via the same shared direction.

d0.text: Paris + France − Berlin and king + woman − man, because both swap a country name for a city name.

d1.text: cat − pet + wild ≈ lion and happy − positive + negative ≈ sad, because emotion and category are also directions.

d2.text: king − queen + princess ≈ prince and man − woman + girl ≈ boy, because subtracting and re-adding within the same category works.

per_question_breakdown.note: 141 > 134.4 — correct answer exceeds 120% of distractor mean by 6.6 chars.

per_question_breakdown.correct_text_stripped: Both arrows represent the same shared direction — the encoded relationship (gender) is geometrically consistent across both word pairs in the space.

d1.text: Arrow A and Arrow B being parallel means the dot product of man and king equals the dot product of woman and queen.

d2.text: The parallel arrows show that man and king are synonyms, and that woman and queen are synonyms, because they share the same coordinate.

d3.text: The arrows being equal length means these four words appear equally often in training text, since frequent words produce longer arrows.

per_question_breakdown.correct_text_stripped: Training data bias is revealed — "doctor" appeared mainly in male contexts and "nurse" in female ones; this is a bias audit finding about the data.

d0.text: The analogy arithmetic has failed for job-title words, since professional terms are too rare for the model to learn reliable gender directions.

d2.text: The analogy cannot apply to job-title words because no gender direction exists for professional terms in an embedding space trained on general text.

d3.text: The result is a confirmed fact showing the model correctly identified that doctors are always male and nurses are always female in the real world.

weakest_questions.distractor: cat − pet + wild ≈ lion and happy − positive + negative ≈ sad, because emotion and category are also directions.
weakest_questions.note: Off-corpus examples a beginner could plausibly construct; wrong but not absurd — plausible analogical reasoning from the topic.

distractor_plausibility.notes: All 18 distractors target genuine beginner misconceptions grounded in corpus content. No trivially absurd option identified. absurd_rate 0/18 = 0.00, well below the 25% threshold. PASS.

coverage_map.text: Define what a direction in embedding space means and give an example of a direction that encodes a real-world relationship.
Explain how the analogy 'king − man + woman ≈ queen' can be described as a sequence of vector arithmetic steps.
Describe why the result of the arithmetic is approximate rather than exact, and what 'nearest word' means in that context.
Identify at least two other analogies that follow the same vector arithmetic pattern.
Interpret a simple 2D diagram showing words as points with arrows, identifying which arrow represents a shared direction.

extra_questions.objective_ref: Applied: interpret a real-world analogy arithmetic result as a bias audit finding (corpus §7).
extra_questions.status: on-scope extension — tests real-world application of vector arithmetic explicitly taught in corpus §7 (Detecting and auditing bias). Not an orphan.

objective_coverage.notes: All 5 LOs covered by at least one question. q6 is an applied extension grounded in corpus §7 with an explicit corpus citation in the sidecar. No orphan questions. PASS.

beginner_scope.notes: All stems use vocabulary defined in this topic or its declared prerequisites (6.3 vectors, 6.5 points in space, 6.6 dot product, 6.7 embeddings). The q5 diagram scenario provides coordinates inline — fully self-contained. q6 doctor/nurse bias scenario is explicitly covered in corpus §7. No unexplained acronyms, no undefined jargon, no forward references requiring outside knowledge. PASS.

verdict: PASS — all checks clean. Positional distribution {0:2,1:2,2:1,3:1}, max_share 0.333. Length-bias rate 0.333 (2/6 flagged: q1 marginal +2 chars, q4 +6.6 chars) — below 0.40 threshold. Zero absurd distractors. All 5 LOs covered. Beginner scope confirmed.
